text: Make the backend test script run the full backend test suite
description: `npm run test -w backend` currently reports green while only executing a subset of backend tests. Fix the backend test command or test discovery so the advertised package-level test script actually covers the intended backend test files.

reflections.context: Make the backend package test command actually run the full backend suite instead of a shell-glob-dependent subset.
reflections.outcome: Replaced the fragile glob-based test script with explicit test-file discovery, then ran the suite sequentially to avoid shared filesystem races. npm run test -w backend now executes all 26 backend tests and passes.
reflections.effectiveness: The approach worked well because it immediately exposed the hidden server.test issue and made the package script honest and reproducible.
reflections.lesson: When a package script claims to run a full suite, verify the discovered file set directly and prefer explicit discovery over shell-dependent globs.
reflections.pattern: Test runners that rely on recursive shell globs often look green while silently skipping files.

status.comment: Fixed. The backend package test script now discovers all backend test files explicitly and runs them sequentially, so npm run test -w backend executes the full suite and passes (26 tests).
